en: Now, I live in New York City, man.
I-I really enjoy it.
This accent, though, I'm out here catching some strays.
There's, uh, there's a lot of projecting going on.
I'll be walking around Manhattan.
I go, y'all have a good afternoon.
They go, we don't want ice in our neighborhood.
I-I go, wow, that-that's from my voice?
Goodness, Greg, I was hoping to inspire y'all
to eat some biscuits, you know?
And I'll tell you, New Yorkers
have my favorite reaction to my accent.
Like, real New Yorkers, they-they can't believe it.
They-when they run into me, and I think it's because
Southerners, we visit New York.
New Yorkers do not visit us.
So when we run into each other, like, when they-
when they run into me, it's like
they've caught a Pokemon, man.
They-they cannot wrap their head around it.
They're like, hey, this guy sounds like Huckleberry Finn.
